prompt:
This is the nursery lot-board module that a contractor split into 'proper' headers right before their contract ended, and it has not compiled since. I am mostly a Python person and the gcc output has me questioning reality. Highlights from `make -k test` (the full thing is pages):

    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c plant.c
    In file included from plant.c:1:
    plant.h:11:33: error: 'struct bed_row' declared inside parameter list will not be visible outside of this definition or declaration [-Werror]
       11 | int bed_free_slots(const struct bed_row *r);
    plant.c:11:5: error: conflicting types for 'bed_free_slots'; have 'int(const struct bed_row *)'
    plant.h:11:5: note: previous declaration of 'bed_free_slots' with type 'int(const struct bed_row *)'
    make: *** [Makefile:8: plant.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c lot.c
    In file included from lot.c:1:
    lot.h:14:53: error: unknown type name 'size_t'
    lot.h:18:27: error: unknown type name 'size_t'
    make: *** [Makefile:11: lot.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_nursery.c
    In file included from lot.h:4,
                     from test_nursery.c:7:
    units.h:3:8: error: redefinition of 'struct bed_row'
    units.h:3:8: note: originally defined here
    test_nursery.c:18:33: error: passing argument 1 of 'bed_free_slots' from incompatible pointer type [-Wincompatible-pointer-types]
    plant.h:11:42: note: expected 'const struct bed_row *' but argument is of type 'const struct bed_row *'
    test_nursery.c:29:28: error: invalid use of undefined type 'struct stock_lev'
    test_nursery.c:29:22: error: storage size of 'lev' isn't known
    make: *** [Makefile:14: test_nursery.o] Error 1

Please just look at that middle note. Expected 'const struct bed_row *' but argument is of type 'const struct bed_row *'. It wants the type it got and it is still angry. Also units.h is apparently 'redefined' and 'originally defined' at the same line of the same file, and size_t — the most basic type C has? — is unknown. None of the actual logic changed in this branch; every function body is character-for-character what was running before the header shuffle, and the tests pin all the maths and the label strings to last season's behavior.

I suspect the headers themselves are wired up wrong somehow but I don't know C well enough to say how. Fair warning from my flailing: some of these only show their real face after you fix something else — lot.c had a second act I never got to the bottom of. Get `make test` green without touching test_nursery.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile, and without changing what anything computes.
notes: 5C4 item 3: header hygiene. gcc 16.1.1, -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror. Five planted defects, all in headers (every .c body is correct and unchanged by the reference fix except deleting the relocated struct): (1) units.h has no include guard — test TU includes it directly and again via lot.h -> 'redefinition of struct bed_row'; (2) plant.h uses struct bed_row without including units.h — '-Werror: declared inside parameter list' in plant.c and the test TU, plus the deliberately disorienting "expected 'const struct bed_row *' but argument is of type 'const struct bed_row *'" incompatible-pointer notes (two identically-spelled but distinct-scope types); (3) struct stock_lev is defined in plant.c but returned by value through plant.h — 'invalid use of undefined type' / 'storage size isn't known' at the frozen test's call site, forcing the definition into a header; (4) lot.h uses struct plant_rec (defined in plant.h) with neither an include nor a forward declaration — fix is 'struct plant_rec;' since only pointers cross the interface; (5) lot.h uses size_t without <stddef.h> ('unknown type name'). CHAIN-REVEAL, documented per 5C law and flagged in the prompt ('lot.c had a second act'): defect (4) is invisible at baseline because gcc stops diagnosing lot.h:14/18 at the unknown size_t; adding <stddef.h> reveals the plant_rec param-list errors on the same lines (probed). Also an INTERACTION: fixing (2) by adding units.h to plant.h makes plant.c double-include units.h, so (1)'s guard becomes load-bearing beyond the test TU. Differentiation from c-grainband (5S4#4, guard TYPO as a syntax lesson): here the guard is absent entirely and the seed's center is multi-header wiring — self-sufficiency, forward declarations, where a by-value struct must live. Reference fix: guard units.h, include units.h + relocate struct stock_lev into plant.h, forward-declare struct plant_rec + include <stddef.h> in lot.h.
